sourceMovie.title: Yes Man
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Yes Man for the same reason it broke through in 2008: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a romance rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Peyton Reed keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Crazy, Stupid, Love.
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Yes Man clicked for you, Crazy, Stupid, Love. (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Yes Man?
faq.answer: Try Crazy, Stupid, Love., 10 Things I Hate About You, Knocked Up—each shares DNA with Yes Man while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Yes Man?
faq.answer: Yes Man (2008) is best described as Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Yes Man worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
